meaning: n.反对,相反adv.反对地,相反地
examples: There is a traffic pattern that we call a contra flow lane.
If any cash is transferred from the office to the bank, a contra entry is made.
Contra Dance studied in this dissertation is a form of dance by pairs of dancers.
Furthermore, contra Bremmer, the extent to which Western banks are fleeing is actually difficult to gauge.
As for Reagan and Clinton, despite Iran-Contra and impeachment, their second terms were mostly policy successes.
